1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 64076443) FILED BY PLAINTIFF PENINSULA CORRIDOR JOINT POWERS BOARD A JOINT POWERS AUTHORITY AS TO DEFENDANT GOLDEN BAY FENCE PLUS IRON WORKS INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO CALTRANS, CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION DOES 1 TO 50

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED ON DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION'S AMENDED DEMURRER AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION'S AMENDED DEMURRER IS OFF CALENDAR. PLAINTIFF PENINSULA CORRIDOR JOINT POWERS BOARD FILED ITS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (FAC) ON AUGUST 8, 2019. (SEE STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND V. SUPERIOR COURT (2010) 184 CALAPP.4TH 1124, 1131 ["THE FILING OF AN AMENDED COMPLAINT MOOTS A MOTION DIRECTED TO A PRIOR COMPLAINT."].) IF, AS THE STATE INDICATES IN ITS SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION, IT DOES NOT BELIEVE THE AMENDMENTS OVERCOME THE DEFECTS IN THE PRIOR COMPLAINT, IT MUST MEET AND CONFER AGAIN WITH PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL AND THEN, IF UNABLE TO RESOLVE THE ISSUES, FILE A NEW DEMURRER. (SEE CODE CIV. PROC. SEC. 430.41 ["IF AN AMENDED COMPLAINT . . . IS FILED, THE RESPONDING PARTY SHALL MEET AND CONFER AGAIN WITH THE PARTY WHO FILED THE AMENDED PLEADING BEFORE FILING A DEMURRER TO THE AMENDED PLEADING."].) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

REPLY PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT STATES DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 64299165) FILED BY DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, BY AND THROUGH THE DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION (CALTRANS)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, BY AND THROUGH THE DEPARTMENT DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 12, 2019. MOVING PARTY IS ORDERED TO COMPLY WITH SLFR 2.7B AND PROVIDE A COURTESY COPY OF THE LATE-FILED RELY NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 18, 2019 WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING NEW HEARING DATE. THE COURT NOTES THAT DEFENDANT HAS BEEN PREVIOUSLY ADVISED THAT COURTESY COPIES HAVE TO BE DELIVERED. WHILE IN ACCORDANCE WITH OCTOBER 7, 2019 MINUTE ORDER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS HAVE BEEN DELIVERED, NO COURTESY COPIES OF THE REPLY HAVE BEEN RECEIVED. FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH PROCEDURAL REQUIREMENTS IN THE FUTURE WILL RESULT IN PAPERS NOT BEING CONSIDERED. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, BY AND THROUGH THE DEPARTMENT DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE (1) BASIS FOR THE MOVING DEFENDANT'S DIRECT LIABILITY; STATUTES CURRENTLY ALLEGED DO NOT PROVIDE SUCH BASIS; AND (2) FACTS GIVING RISE TO VICARIOUS LIABILITY. ALLEGATIONS IN PARAGRAPH 29 "DEFENDANTS AND THEIR EMPLOYEES." IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION AND DOES NOT SPECIFY EMPLOYS OF WHICH DEFENDANT (OR EVEN THAT EMPLOYEES OF EACH DEFENDANT) COMMITTED TRESPASS AS THIS ALLEGATION LUMPS ALL DEFENDANTS TOGETHER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; REPORTED: DEBORAH FUQUA, 415.810.7170, DFUQUA@GAMIL.COM. (501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-12-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Laurel O'Connor, 415.777.3200 for Plaintiff Peninsula Corridor Joint Powers Board.
Yuping Lin, 510.433.9100 for Defendant Golden Bay Fence Plus Iron Works, Inc.
Reported: Deborah Fuqua, 415.810.7170, dfuqua@gamil.com.

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, BY AND THROUGH THE DEPARTMENT DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege (1) basis for the moving defendant's direct liability; statutes currently alleged do not provide such basis; and (2) facts giving rise to vicarious liability. Allegations in paragraph 29 "defendants and their employees." is not sufficient to state a cause of action and does not specify employs of which defendant (or even that employees of each defendant) committed trespass as this allegation lumps all defendants together. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Reported: Deborah Fuqua, 415.810.7170, dfuqua@gamil.com. (501)

Off Calendar Oct-15-2019 Continued To Nov-12-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, BY AND THROUGH THE DEPARTMENT DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to November 12, 2019. Moving party is ordered to comply with SLFR 2.7B and provide a courtesy copy of the late-filed rely no later than October 18, 2019 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. The Court notes that Defendant has been previously advised that courtesy copies have to be delivered. While in accordance with October 7, 2019 minute order courtesy copies of the moving papers have been delivered, no courtesy copies of the reply have been received. Failure to comply with procedural requirements in the future will result in papers not being considered. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (501)

DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA, BY AND THROUGH THE DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION (CALTRANS), DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is transferred to be heard in department 501 on October 15, 2019, at 9:30 a.m., per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. AMENDED DEMURRER
Off Calendar Aug-22-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

There being no appearance, the tentative ruling is adopted on DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF TRANSPORTATION'S AMENDED DEMURRER as follows:

Defendant State of California Department of Transportation's amended demurrer is OFF CALENDAR.

Plaintiff Peninsula Corridor Joint Powers Board filed its first amended complaint (FAC) on August 8, 2019. (See State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 CalApp.4th 1124, 1131 ["the filing of an amended complaint moots a motion directed to a prior complaint."].) If, as the State indicates in its supplemental declaration, it does not believe the amendments overcome the defects in the prior complaint, it must meet and confer again with Plaintiff's counsel and then, if unable to resolve the issues, file a new demurrer. (See Code Civ. Proc. Sec. 430.41 ["If an amended complaint . . . is filed, the responding party shall meet and confer again with the party who filed the amended pleading before filing a demurrer to the amended pleading."].)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.
